title: Vaama Duraiyamma song lyrics -Madharasapattinam
stanzas:
Singers: Udit Narayan, 
Voice:    VMC Haneefa, Amy Jackson

Vaama Duraiyamma
---hmmmm? .. Common White lady
Idhu vangak kariyamma...
---What?. Padraram singing..

Vaama Duraiyamma
Idhu vangak kariyamma...
Vanakam sollithan varaverkum ooramma
--- we welcome with vanakam
--- Ohhh vanakkam
Kattavandiyilum povom, tromil eriyum povom
Koovam padagilum povom, polamaaa....
Magudi oodhida paambu aadudhey yamma ..
--Snake Dance
Yaanai thumbikai aasirvadhangal yamma ..
--Elephant hands
Odiya dhiyam ingae yammamma...
-- WOW

Ohhhh pavai koothukal bommalatangal
kovil sirpathil kalai vazharpom
Dhinam vaasal kolathil arisi maviley
Pravaikum erumbukum viruindhu vaipom
-- Whats this?
-- Food for koruvi's
-- Oh really
-- yes
Kodi jadhigal ingae ullapodhilum
Annan thambiyaai nangal vazhvom
-- all are brothers and sisters but parents different
-- great
-- thank  you thank you
Veetil thinnaigal kattuvom yamma...
Vazhipokan vandhudhan thoongi selluvan summa...
Thayum deivamthan ingae yammammaa...
--Lovely

Ohh kodi aandugal thandi vazhdhidum
senthamizh engal mozhiyaagum
Ada kamban valluvan kavidhaiyil sonna
Vazhkai engal neriyaagum..
-- Who is that?
-- adhu yaarunnu sonna puriyava pogudhu, old poets written gold lines
Indha bumiyil neengal engum pogalam
Ingu mattume anbai kanalam
-- I Love You
-- I beg your pardon
-- No No land of love, thapichonda sami
Veera mannargal vazhdha nadidhu yamma..
Idhai adimaiyaakithan kodumai seivadhu niyayama....
-- yeah summa irappa enpolappa keduthiradha
Valyil mazhayum than vizhudhadhu yammamma ..
-- ammamma

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

Vaama Duraiyamma is a lively song that celebrates welcoming and joy, reflecting the cultural essence of the movie.

What is the plot context of the movie?

Madharasapattinam is set in the backdrop of pre-independence India, exploring themes of love and cultural identity.

Who is the singer of the song?

The song is sung by the talented Udit Narayan, known for his melodious voice in Tamil cinema.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Vaama Duraiyamma was composed by GV PrakashKumar, a prominent figure in Tamil film music.

When was the song released?

The song was released on April 9, 2010.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Vaama Duraiyamma, the characters express their joy and excitement through a lively celebration, perfectly encapsulating the spirit of the film Madharasapattinam. The upbeat tempo and catchy lyrics invite listeners to join in the festivities, making it an ideal track for celebrations and parties. The song conveys a sense of warmth and community, evoking feelings of happiness and togetherness. With Udit Narayan's energetic vocals and GV PrakashKumar's vibrant composition, this song stands out as a memorable highlight of the movie.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Vaama Duraiyamma from Madharasapattinam is a vibrant celebration of joy and energy, brilliantly brought to life by Udit Narayan's captivating vocals. The infectious beats crafted by GV PrakashKumar perfectly complement the lively lyrics penned by Na Muthukumar. This song is sure to uplift your spirits and get you dancing along!
stanzaMeanings: What? This is a common white lady's thing...

We welcome with greetings. Oh, greetings! We will go to the place of the festival, we will go to the fire in the drum, we will go to the singing. The snake dances, oh! The elephant blesses with its trunk. Wow!

What is this? Food for the little birds? Oh really? Yes, we will live here as brothers and sisters, but with different parents. Great! Thank you, thank you! We will eat at home... The one who comes to worship will just sleep... Mother and God are here... Lovely.

Oh, the rooster will live and our Tamil language will flourish. Who is that? I don't understand who that is. Old poets wrote golden lines. You can go anywhere in this world. Here, only love can be seen. I love you. I beg your pardon. No, no, this is a land of love. The brave will live here. Is it fair to make this a slavery? Yeah, just like that, the rain fell on the ground.
